title: onobrychis viciifolia subsp. altissima
intro: Onobrychis viciifolia subsp. altissima, commonly known as sainfoin, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of sainfoin include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. Sainfoin can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb for use in herbal remedies, and it is also used as a nutritious feed for livestock due to its high protein content and ability to fix nitrogen in the soil.
